approach: Write the literal text "hello anvil" to test.txt in the target repo.
expected_outcome: test.txt exists in the target repo containing "hello anvil".

scope_boundaries.out_of_scope: Any other file; any operation beyond write/smoke-test/commit.

escalation_triggers: If test.txt already exists with unexpected content (the trivial brief assumes a fresh /tmp repo).

approach: Append the literal text " world" to the existing test.txt, yielding "hello anvil world".

scope_boundaries.out_of_scope: Any other file; recreating test.txt from scratch.

escalation_triggers: If step 1 did not leave test.txt as "hello anvil" (append would produce wrong content).

approach: Read test.txt and confirm it is exactly "hello anvil world"; make a no-op commit (mtime bump) to mark the build complete. No content write — step 3's declared scope.operations is read/smoke-test/commit.
smoke_test: grep -q "hello anvil world" test.txt && echo pass
expected_outcome: test.txt reads "hello anvil world"; a final no-op commit is recorded.

scope_boundaries.out_of_scope: Any write/modification of test.txt or any other file (write is NOT in step 3's declared scope).

escalation_triggers: If test.txt content is not exactly "hello anvil world" — a prior step misbehaved; do not paper over it.
